Title: PpNAC1, a main regulator of phenylalanine biosynthesis and utilization in maritime pine

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NAC transcription factor; VNS family; NST group
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phenylalanine is a precursor for phenylpropanoids; lignin contributes to secondary cell wall formation and wood formation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes phenylalanine biosynthesis and utilization, activating the transcriptional network leading to lignin/secondary cell wall formation; silencing reduces expression of phenylalanine and phenylpropanoid pathway genes and alters vascular patterning
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Main upstream regulator/master regulator of phenylalanine utilization and secondary cell wall-associated phenylpropanoid metabolism in maritime pine
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly activates its own promoter and the PpMyb4 promoter; indirectly promotes PpMyb8 and downstream phenylpropanoid/lignin genes
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: biosynthesis and utilization of phenylalanine; secondary cell wall biosynthesis; lignin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Positive feedback/autoregulation by binding its own promoter SNBE sites
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predominantly expressed in secondary xylem and compression wood of adult trees; also high in roots and hypocotyls of seedlings and in needles, stems and roots of 1-year-old trees
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in tissues undergoing secondary wall thickening; strongest in secondary xylem and compression wood
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Compression wood formation; tissues undergoing secondary wall thickening
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: RNAi driven by maize UBIL constitutive promoter; effector constructs under CaMV 35S constitutive promoter in protoplast assays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi silencing in P. pinaster; qPCR; microarray transcriptomics (GSE89341); histology; EMSA; promoter transactivation in pine protoplasts; heterologous overexpression in Arabidopsis
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing caused poor growth, delayed development, thickened hypocotyls and altered vascular radial patterning
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to manipulate wood formation and lignin-related traits in conifer biotechnology, but dosage must be controlled to avoid growth defects
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpNAC1 is an NST-group NAC TF highly expressed in secondary xylem and compression wood. 2. Silencing PpNAC1 disrupts vascular development and down-regulates genes involved in amino acid metabolism, phenylpropanoid/lignin biosynthesis, and secondary cell wall formation. 3. PpNAC1 directly binds and activates its own promoter and the PpMyb4 promoter, placing it at the top of a transcriptional cascade controlling phenylalanine utilization for wood formation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as a functional ortholog candidate of Arabidopsis SND1/NST1; proposed first-level regulator in conifer phenylalanine metabolism linked to wood formation

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports secondary cell wall strengthening and wood formation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates PpMyb8 expression and functions as an activator of lignin biosynthesis downstream of PpNAC1
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Intermediate regulator linking PpNAC1 to PpMyb8 and lignin biosynthetic activation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly activates the PpMyb8 promoter through AC-II elements
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: phenylalanine utilization into phenylpropanoid/lignin biosynthesis; secondary cell wall formation
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Feedforward transcriptional cascade downstream of PpNAC1
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Associated with secondary xylem; transcript reduced in PpNAC1 RNAi plantlets
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Effector construct under CaMV 35S constitutive promoter in protoplast assays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR in PpNAC1_RNAi lines; EMSA; promoter transactivation in pine protoplasts
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering node for modulating lignin/wood properties, likely best used with upstream regulators in coordinated strategies
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpMyb4 expression decreases upon PpNAC1 silencing. 2. PpNAC1 directly activates the PpMyb4 promoter. 3. PpMyb4 directly binds the PpMyb8 promoter and activates its expression, placing PpMyb4 in the middle of the transcriptional cascade for lignin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper concludes PpMyb4 is not entirely lignin-specific and may also participate in cellulose/xylan-related regulation based on ortholog information

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports lignification, secondary cell wall formation and wood development
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts downstream in the transcriptional cascade to promote lignin biosynthesis and phenylalanine pathway gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Downstream activator of phenylalanine/lignin biosynthetic genes in maritime pine
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: genes involved in phenylalanine and lignin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activated by PpMyb4; previously shown to regulate phenylalanine pathway/lignin biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: phenylalanine biosynthesis and utilization; lignin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Activated by PpMyb4; indirectly dependent on PpNAC1
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in secondary xylem; strongly reduced in PpNAC1 RNAi plantlets
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Reporter promoter tested in protoplasts; activation by CaMV 35S:PpMyb4 effector
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR in PpNAC1_RNAi lines; EMSA and transactivation for regulation by PpMyb4; previous functional evidence cited for pathway regulation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful downstream target for fine-tuning lignin biosynthesis in wood biotechnology
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpMyb8 expression is reduced when PpNAC1 is silenced. 2. PpMyb4 directly binds AC-II elements in the PpMyb8 promoter and activates it. 3. PpMyb8 functions downstream in the conifer transcriptional cascade regulating phenylalanine and lignin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Its direct metabolic role is based partly on previous work; in this paper the core new evidence is its position downstream of PpMyb4 in the regulatory cascade
